pattern: unvalidated CLI arg in glob path
description: CLI arg $session_id used unvalidated in glob path; crafted ../value could reference files outside recordings directory
suggested_fix: Validate $session_id matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ before interpolation

description: DOCKERHUB_TOKEN sourced from env file and piped to docker login --password-stdin; token visible in process environment
suggested_fix: Use a dedicated CI secret store rather than a plain .env file; ensure .env is in .gitignore

pattern: git clone external URL without hash pin
description: git clone fetches from https://github.com/openai/codex.git at HEAD with no commit SHA verification
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific commit SHA: git clone ... && git -C $TMP_DIR checkout <SHA>

description: curl health check to http://localhost:5175; localhost-only, no external data transfer
fp_reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

description: curl health check to http://localhost:$port; localhost-only, no external data transfer
fp_reason: curl targets localhost only; no external network exposure or data exfiltration possible
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish between localhost health checks and external network calls to avoid noise

description: Dependency the-companion uses ^0.2.2; patch and minor updates apply automatically on install
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or ensure bun.lock is committed and CI uses --frozen-lockfile

description: DevDependency husky uses ^9.1.7; automatic updates could introduce breaking changes in git hooks
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version or ensure lockfile is committed

description: Skill does not specify what format the invoking agent should produce; output format varies between invocations
suggested_fix: Add an ## Output Format section describing the expected structure of changes or summary the skill should produce

description: 'where appropriate' used twice (lines 54 and 83) without measurable criteria for what constitutes appropriate use
suggested_fix: Replace with specific conditions, e.g., 'on lists with 3+ items' instead of 'where appropriate'

description: 'Appropriate motion: Motion serves purpose, not decoration' lacks measurable criteria for what purpose means
suggested_fix: Specify concrete motion purposes, e.g., 'state-change feedback, entrance/exit, loading indication'

description: 'MUST STOP and STOP' is a doubled imperative; 'to clarify. whether you got it right' has a mid-sentence period — copy-paste template error wastes tokens and may confuse the agent
suggested_fix: Fix to: 'you MUST STOP and call the AskUserQuestionTool to clarify whether you got it right'

description: Seven skills instruct agents to 'call the AskUserQuestionTool' — this is not a standard Claude Code tool and will silently fail in Claude Code context
suggested_fix: Document AskUserQuestionTool as a custom tool registration requirement, or replace with a standard ask-user clarification pattern

description: CLAUDE.md instructs 'Always use agent-browser CLI command to explore the browser' but agent-browser is not a standard Claude Code tool and is not defined in this repo
suggested_fix: Document how to install agent-browser or replace with the actual CLI invocation or tool name
